content.text:
You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/console-plugin/source/src/components/logs/TektonTaskRunLog.scss

Apply ALL of the following 4 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 6
Rule [sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-log-viewer]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-log-viewer' was removed in the new version. A simple version prefix swap to 'pf-v6-c-log-viewer' does NOT produce a valid class — this class does not exist in the target CSS distribution.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a PatternFly component prop.

Incident context:
  className: pf-v5-c-log-viewer

Code context:
```
    5  
    6    .pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text {
    7      white-space: pre;

```

### Fix 2
Line: 6
Rule [sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-log-viewer-text]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text' was removed in the new version. A simple version prefix swap to 'pf-v6-c-log-viewer__text' does NOT produce a valid class — this class does not exist in the target CSS distribution.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a PatternFly component prop.

Incident context:
  className: pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text

Code context:
```
    5  
    6    .pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text {
    7      white-space: pre;

```

### Fix 3
Line: 6
Rule [semver-css-class-removed-c-log-viewer]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-log-viewer' has no equivalent in the new version. There is no valid 'pf-v6-c-log-viewer' class — this class was removed, not renamed.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a component prop.

Incident context:
  className: pf-v5-c-log-viewer

Code context:
```
    5  
    6    .pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text {
    7      white-space: pre;

```

### Fix 4
Line: 6
Rule [semver-css-class-removed-c-log-viewer-text]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text' has no equivalent in the new version. There is no valid 'pf-v6-c-log-viewer__text' class — this class was removed, not renamed.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a component prop.

Incident context:
  className: pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text

Code context:
```
    5  
    6    .pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text {
    7      white-space: pre;

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/console-plugin/source/src/components/logs/TektonTaskRunLog.scss
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand its current content.

Now let me reason through each fix:

**Fix 1 & Fix 3**: Both target line 6, saying `pf-v5-c-log-viewer` (the parent BEM block portion of the selector) was removed and has no v6 equivalent.

**Fix 2 & Fix 4**: Both target line 6, saying `pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text` (the full BEM class) was removed and has no v6 equivalent.

All four fixes converge on the same thing: the CSS selector `.pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text` on line 6 references classes that no longer exist in PatternFly v6. The entire rule block (lines 6-8) uses a dead selector.

The purpose of this rule was to set `white-space: pre` on elements with the `.pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text` class within `.odc-tekton-taskrun-log`. Since this class no longer exists in PF v6, the rule will never match anything — it's dead CSS.

The correct fix is to remove this dead CSS rule. Since this is a custom SCSS override targeting a PatternFly internal class, and the LogViewer component in PF v6 likely handles text formatting differently (or uses different class names), removing the dead selector is the right approach.

After removing lines 6-8, I should also clean up the empty line that would remain (line 5 before `@include`).
Let me verify the final file:

The file looks correct. The dead `.pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text` rule block has been removed, and the remaining SCSS structure is clean and valid.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Detail |
|-----|------|--------|--------|
| Fix 1 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-log-viewer` | ✅ Applied | Removed the `.pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text { white-space: pre; }` rule block (lines 6-8) which referenced the dead `pf-v5-c-log-viewer` BEM block class that has no PF v6 equivalent. |
| Fix 2 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-log-viewer-text` | ✅ Applied | Same removal — the full `pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text` class was the selector of the removed rule block. |
| Fix 3 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-log-viewer` | ✅ Applied | Same removal — the `pf-v5-c-log-viewer` parent block portion of the selector was removed along with the rule. |
| Fix 4 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-log-viewer-text` | ✅ Applied | Same removal — the `pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text` class reference was eliminated by removing the dead rule block. |

All four fixes targeted the same CSS rule block (`.pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text { white-space: pre; }`) on line 6. The rule was removed entirely since both the BEM block (`pf-v5-c-log-viewer`) and element (`pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text`) classes no longer exist in PatternFly v6 and have no equivalent replacements. The surrounding SCSS structure (`.odc-tekton-taskrun-log` with `overflow: hidden` and the `@include ansi-log-colors` mixin) remains intact and functional.

| Fix | Rule | Status | Detail |

| Fix 1 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-log-viewer` | ✅ Applied | Removed the `.pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text { white-space: pre; }` rule block (lines 6-8) which referenced the dead `pf-v5-c-log-viewer` BEM block class that has no PF v6 equivalent. |

| Fix 4 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-log-viewer-text` | ✅ Applied | Same removal — the `pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text` class reference was eliminated by removing the dead rule block. |

All four fixes targeted the same CSS rule block (`.pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text { white-space: pre; }`) on line 6.
